revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:御霊神社 (鎌倉市)]] {{Shrine | Name = Goryō Shrine (御霊神社) | image = [[File:Goryou jinnja 002.jpg|260px|Goryō Shrine]]
Worship Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|18|40.0|N|139|31|58.0|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}} {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|qid=Q200267|lt=Kamakura City|1=Kamakura}} {{ill|Sakanoshita|ja|坂ノ下 (鎌倉市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoshita|1=Sakanoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21653160}} | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 18 | Latitude seconds = 40.0 | Longitude degrees = 139 | Longitude minutes = 31 | Longitude seconds = 58.0 | deity = {{ill|Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|en|Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|qid=Q1154948|lt=Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|1=Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | Founded = Unknown (Estimated late Heian period) | Alias = (Kamakura) Gongorō Shrine (鎌倉 権五郎神社) | Pilgrimage route = {{ill|Kamakura Enoshima Shichifukujin|ja|鎌倉江の島七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Enoshima Shichifukujin|1=Kamakura Enoshima Shichifukujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17225980}} (Fukurokuju) | Annual festival = {{ill|September 18|en|September 18|qid=Q2893|lt=September 18|1=September 18}} (See [[#Festivals|Festivals]] section) | Ceremonies = July (See [[#Festivals|Festivals]] section) | Map = Japan Kanagawa | Label position = bottom }} '''Goryō Shrine''' (御霊神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|qid=Q200267|lt=Kamakura City|1=Kamakura}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. Under the former shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a {{ill|Former Village Shrine|ja|旧村社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. There are two shrines named Goryō Shrine in Kamakura City; this article focuses on the one in {{ill|Sakanoshita|ja|坂ノ下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoshita|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 坂ノ下 (鎌倉市)|1=Sakanoshita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21653160}}, commonly known as '''(Kamakura) Gongorō Shrine''' (（鎌倉）権五郎神社). == History == The exact founding date of Goryō Shrine is unknown. However, considering the spread of {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|qid=Q3273210|lt=Goryō belief|1=Goryō}} and the regional development efforts by the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11650931}}, it is estimated to have been established during the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. Originally, it enshrined the spirits of five major families of the {{ill|Kamakura Party|ja|鎌倉党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉氏|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11650931}}, namely the {{ill|Kamakura clan|ja|鎌倉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura clan|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11650931}}, {{ill|Kajiwara clan|ja|梶原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kajiwara clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Muraoka clan|ja|村岡氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraoka clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nagao clan|en|Nagao clan|qid=Q1655561|lt=Nagao clan|1=Nagao clan}}, and {{ill|Ōba clan|ja|大庭氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōba clan|1=Ōba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11435393}}. It was called "Goryō Shrine" (御霊神社) meaning "Five Spirits Shrine" from these five clans. Later, the enshrinement was consolidated to {{ill|Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|en|Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|qid=Q1154948|lt=Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|1=Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa}} alone, leading to the name "Gongorō Shrine" (権五郎神社). Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa, a warrior of the {{ill|Kamakura Party|ja|鎌倉党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura Party|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鎌倉氏|1=Kamakura clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11650931}} in the late Heian period, was known for his bravery. At age 16, he fought under {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshiie|en|Minamoto no Yoshiie|qid=Q32613|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|1=Minamoto no Yoshiie}} in the {{ill|Gosannen War|en|Gosannen War|qid=Q1187411|lt=Later Three-Year War|1=Gosannen War}} (1083–1087). According to the ''{{ill|Scroll of the Latter Three Years' War|en|Scroll of the Latter Three Years' War|qid=Q3594051|lt=Ōshū Gosannenki|1=Scroll of the Latter Three Years' War}}'', he was struck in the left eye by an enemy arrow but continued to fight and defeated his assailant. When a fellow warrior attempted to remove the arrow by placing a foot on his face, Kagemasa scolded him for disrespecting a samurai. This legend associates Goryō Shrine with virtues of academic success and victory. Additionally, according to {{ill|Hayashi Razan|en|Hayashi Razan|qid=Q558279|lt=Hayashi Razan|1=Hayashi Razan}}'s ''{{ill|Honchō Jinjako|ja|本朝神社考|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honchō Jinjako|1=Honchō Jinja-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11520404}}'', the shrine is also believed to cure eye diseases. File:P4280084 Sacred tablet.JPG|Plaque on the main hall File:Goryou jinnja 003.jpg|Path through Sakanoshita residential area File:Goryou jinnja 001.jpg|The {{ill|Enoshima Electric Railway|en|Enoshima Electric Railway|qid=Q250760|lt=Enoden|1=Enoshima Electric Railway}} line passes just in front of the shrine grounds == Shrine Grounds == === Auxiliary Shrines (境内社) === * {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q514480}} – Enshrines {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}}, the deity of food and harvests. Transferred from {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|en|Fushimi Inari-taisha|qid=Q714828|lt=Fushimi Inari Taisha|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha}}. * {{ill|Akiba Shrine|ja|秋葉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiba Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrines {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|qid=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}, the fire god. Erected following a major fire in Sakanoshita during the Meiji era, with prayers for fire prevention. The deity was transferred from {{ill|Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine|en|Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine|qid=Q500413|lt=Akiba Shrines|1=Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine}} in {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|qid=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu City|1=Hamamatsu}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. * {{ill|Dairokuten Shrine|ja|第六天社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairokuten Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 第六天神社}} * {{ill|Chijinsha|ja|地神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chijinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Soreisha|ja|祖霊社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soreisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Honors parishioners who died in the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|en|First Sino-Japanese War|qid=Q178687|lt=First Sino-Japanese War|1=First Sino-Japanese War}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|qid=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War|1=Russo-Japanese War}}, and {{ill|World War I|en|World War I|qid=Q361|lt=World War I|1=World War I}}. * {{ill|Mitake Shrine|ja|御嶽神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitake Shrine|1=Mitake shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11488964}} * {{ill|Ishigami Shrine|ja|石上神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishigami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Dedicated to a boulder off the coast that caused shipwrecks. Fishermen enshrined a part of the rock to pray for maritime safety and abundant catches. The shrine holds its own annual festival. File:P4280066_Inari_little_shrine.JPG|Inari Shrine File:P4280068 Akiba little_shrine.JPG|Akiba Shrine File:P4280088_Soreisha_little_shrine.JPG|Soreisha (Ancestor Shrine) File:P4280092_Dairokutensha_little_shrine.JPG|Dairokuten Shrine File:P4280097_Jijinsha_little_shrine.JPG|Chijinsha File:P4280098_Ishigami_little_shrine.JPG|Ishigami Shrine File:P4280094_Mitake_shrine.JPG|Mitake Shrine === Tabu Tree (タブノキ) === A {{ill|Machilus thunbergii|en|Machilus thunbergii|qid=Q5792073|lt=Tabu tree|1=Machilus thunbergii}} in front of the shrine office, estimated to be 350 years old and 20 meters tall, spreads a magnificent canopy. It is designated a {{ill|Natural Monument of Kamakura City|ja|鎌倉市指定天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural Monument of Kamakura City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and one of the "100 Famous Trees of Kanagawa" selected in December 1984. File:P4280105 Persea tree.JPG|The Tabu tree on the shrine grounds == Festivals == * {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|qid=Q2150|lt=January 1|1=January 1}} – New Year Festival (元旦祭) * {{ill|July 25|en|July 25|qid=Q2720|lt=Sunday closest to July 25|1=July 25}} – Ishigami Shrine Annual Festival (石上神社festival), also known as the ''Gokunagashi Shinji'' (御供流し神事). This ceremony calms the sea gods and memorializes victims of marine accidents by offering red rice (sekihan). A pre-festival (神幸祭) takes place on July 20, where young men swim out to sea from the shrine carrying offerings before placing them on a boat and releasing them into the sea. * {{ill|September 17|en|September 17|qid=Q2894|lt=September 17|1=September 17}} – Respect for the Aged Festival (敬老祭) and Evening Festival (宵宮祭) * {{ill|September 18|en|September 18|qid=Q2893|lt=September 18|1=September 18}} – Main Festival (festival), featuring the famous ''{{ill|Menkake Gyoretsu|ja|面掛行列|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menkake Gyoretsu|1=Menkake Gyōretsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11663791}}'' (Masked Procession), a Kanagawa Prefecture-designated intangible cultural asset, and the ''Kamakura Kagura'' (鎌倉神楽). [[File:Menkake Gyoretsu - Haramitto.jpg|thumbnail|200px|Menkake Gyoretsu: The main character ''Haramitto'' (Okame) and the midwife]] == Access == * Short walk from {{ill|Hase Station|en|Hase Station|qid=Q845426|lt=Hase Station (Kanagawa)|1=Hase Station}} or {{ill|Gokurakuji Station|en|Gokurakuji Station|qid=Q858997|lt=Gokurakuji Station|1=Gokurakuji Station}} on the {{ill|Enoshima Electric Railway|en|Enoshima Electric Railway|qid=Q250760|lt=Enoshima Electric Railway|1=Enoshima Electric Railway}} (Enoden) line. == Related Shrines (同系列の神社) == Numerous Goryō Shrines with similar origins exist throughout Kanagawa Prefecture: * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Kajiwara, Kamakura)|ja|御霊神社 (鎌倉市梶原)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Kajiwara, Kamakura)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504382|1=Goryō Shrine (Kajiwara, Kamakura)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja|御霊神社 (川崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Kawasaki City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504389|1=Goryō Shrine (Kawasaki City)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Kawana, Fujisawa)|ja|御霊神社 (藤沢市川名)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Kawana, Fujisawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504391|1=Goryō Shrine (Kawana, Fujisawa)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Miyamae, Fujisawa)|ja|御霊神社 (藤沢市宮前)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Miyamae, Fujisawa)|qq=}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Chigasaki City)|ja|御霊神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Chigasaki City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504393|1=Goryō Shrine (Chigasaki City)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Fujino Town)|ja|御霊神社 (藤野町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Fujino Town)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504395|1=Goryō Shrine (Fujino Town)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Hayama Town)|ja|御霊神社 (葉山町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Hayama Town)|1=Goryō Shrine (Hayama Town)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11489362}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Yokosuka City)|ja|御霊神社 (横須賀市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Yokosuka City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504398|1=Goryō Shrine (Yokosuka City)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Yokohama City)|ja|御霊社 (横浜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Yokohama City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504400|1=Goryō Shrine (Yokohama City)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Sakae Ward, Yokohama)|ja|御霊神社 (横浜市栄区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Sakae Ward, Yokohama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504402|1=Goryō Shrine (Kanai, Yokohama)}} * {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Izumi Ward, Yokohama)|ja|御霊神社 (横浜市泉区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Izumi Ward, Yokohama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504404|1=Goryō Shrine (Izumi Ward, Yokohama)}} == Additional Information == [[File:Goryou jinnja 501.jpg|thumb|Goryō Shrine Goshuin (shrine stamp)]] {{ill|Keisuke Kuwata|en|Keisuke Kuwata|qid=Q1197175|lt=Keisuke Kuwata|1=Keisuke Kuwata}} of {{ill|Southern All Stars|en|Southern All Stars|qid=Q1378545|lt=Southern All Stars|1=Southern All Stars}} often strolls around Kamakura, including this shrine, which has inspired his lyrics. In an interview published in the ''{{ill|Shūkan Bunshun|en|Shūkan Bunshun|qid=Q4022413|lt=Weekly Bunshun|1=Shūkan Bunshun}}'' (September 18, 2014 issue), he visited Goryō Shrine and was photographed enjoying ''Gongorō Mochi'' from the nearby {{ill|Chikarimochiya|ja|力餅屋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikarimochiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} confectionery''{{ill|Shūkan Bunshun|en|Shūkan Bunshun|qid=Q4022413|lt=Weekly Bunshun|1=Shūkan Bunshun}}'' September 18, 2014, "I Love Pops and Nippon" interview, p.18–19.. == References == == Sources == * Yoshida Shigeho (2002). ''Kamakura no Jinja Kojiten''. Kamakura Shunjūsha. ISBN 4-7740-0205-4 * [https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=398&cd=1205011 Kanagawa Shrine Association – Goryō Shrine] * Shrine history board on-site == See Also == * {{ill|Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|en|Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|qid=Q1154948|lt=Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa|1=Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa}} * {{ill|Shibaraku|en|Shibaraku|qid=Q3821885|lt=Shibaraku (kabuki)|1=Shibaraku}} (Kabuki play) * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Kanagawa|ja|神奈川県の神社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Kanagawa|1=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Office Designated Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588941}} * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Kamakura|ja|鎌倉市内の神社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Kamakura|1=list of Shinto shrines in Kamakura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11650899}} * {{ill|Chikarimochiya|ja|力餅屋 (鎌倉市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikarimochiya|1=Chikaramochiya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97636197}} – Famous for "Gongorō Mochi" == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Goryō-jinja (Kamakura)}} * [https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1205011-000/ Goryō Shrine] – Kanagawa Shrine Association * [https://www.trip-kamakura.com/place/japanheritage/148.html Goryō Shrine] – Kamakura City Tourism Association * [https://kanagawajumokuikai.jp/wp/wp-content/uploads/hpb-media/017.pdf Kanagawa’s 100 Famous Trees – Goryō Shrine Tabu Tree] (2007) }} {{translated page|ja|御霊神社 (鎌倉市)|version=103485896|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{Commons category|Goryō-jinja (Kamakura)}} [[ja:御霊神社 (鎌倉市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11489364}} [[en:Goryō Shrine (Kamakura City)]] [[Category:Kamakura Clan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kamakura, Kanagawa]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
